steps.title: Find the roots
steps.content: For $x^2+49x+1=0$ apply the quadratic formula with $a=1$, $b=49$, $c=1$: $$x=\frac{-49\pm\sqrt{49^2-4(1)(1)}}{2(1)}.$$

steps.title: Identify a valid solution
steps.content: Taking the $+$ branch gives $x=\frac{-49+\sqrt{(49)^{2}-4(1)(1)}}{2(1)}$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
